title: Run L0 static validation and AI issue scan

requirements: Validate 4 contract groups: [commit coverage mapping, tool exposure, docs/runtime agreement, target-repo safety assumptions]
Run 4 analysis classes: [static command review, changed-surface verification, AI issue scan, execution blocker classification]
Classify findings into 3 severities: [must-fix-before-e2e, can-fix-during-IMPL-002, informational]
Verify 2 protocol tools remain primary in the live contract: [fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections]
acceptance: 4 contract groups are explicitly reviewed: verify by producing findings for [coverage mapping, tool exposure, docs/runtime agreement, safety assumptions]
3 commit surfaces are mapped to concrete validation commands: verify by matching [c4d957a -> init/schema, c7b1988 -> plan-context/sections/audit/doctor, 383fefc -> docs contract]
2 tool-exposure checks are completed: verify by confirming fab_plan_context and fab_get_rule_sections appear in registration/docs scans
1 blocker list with 3 severity buckets is produced: verify by enumerating [must-fix-before-e2e, can-fix-during-IMPL-002, informational]
0 write-heavy commands are scheduled for /mnt/c/Project/oops-framework during this task: verify by reviewing the task output and command list

context: Read-only versus disposable-copy safety split is already defined

shared_context.risk_strategy: Escalate contract drift before live E2E and keep original oops-framework repo read-only
conventions: Prefer code-and-test reality over stale planning assumptions
Classify blockers before running the disposable-copy flow
Do not modify production code during static validation

artifacts.usage: Use the L0 plan, risk list, and scenario inventory as the baseline for static validation
artifacts.contains: coverage gaps, AI issue scan risks, and 15 E2E scenarios

artifacts.usage: Map changed files and commands to the validation surface
artifacts.contains: commit-to-path inventory and target-repo safety notes

pre_analysis.action: Load context package to map changed commits and target-repo constraints
Load the L0-L3 test analysis and AI-code issue scan risks
Search for the current plan-context and rule-sections contract across code and docs
Confirm the focused Vitest entrypoints that must pass before live E2E

implementation_approach.title: Map changed functionality to validation coverage
implementation_approach.description: Cross-check the 3 validation commits against existing tests, tool registration, and docs to find missing proof points.
modification_points: Check 4 contract groups: [coverage mapping, tool exposure, docs/runtime agreement, safety assumptions]
logic_flow: Read commit/path inventory from the context package
Match each changed surface to tests and docs
Highlight gaps where runtime proof is still missing
Separate static-only gaps from live-E2E gaps

implementation_approach.title: Run AI issue scan and blocker classification
implementation_approach.description: Review the plan for root-test blind spots, token lifecycle risk, target-repo safety risk, and docs/runtime drift before execution.
modification_points: Classify findings into 3 severities: [must-fix-before-e2e, can-fix-during-IMPL-002, informational]
Review 4 risk classes: [test blind spot, token lifecycle, target dirty state, docs/runtime drift]
Produce 1 blocker handoff for the quality gate task
logic_flow: Apply the AI issue scan risk list from test analysis
Determine whether each finding blocks execution or can be handled during IMPL-002
Document exact blocker wording and evidence
Pass the blocker inventory to the review gate

implementation_approach.title: Emit validation findings for downstream gate
implementation_approach.description: Finalize a concise static-validation report that the quality gate can consume without rerunning the same analysis.
modification_points: Produce 1 static validation summary with 3 severity buckets
logic_flow: Compile coverage mapping and issue scan findings
Verify all commands in this task are read-only
Pass the result forward as gate input

test_commands.run_tests: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run src/index.test.ts src/tools/rule-sections.test.ts src/services/doctor.test.ts && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared exec vitest run test/agents-meta.test.ts
test_commands.run_coverage: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run src/index.test.ts src/tools/rule-sections.test.ts src/services/doctor.test.ts --coverage
